danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen hostile entities in real-time combat zones
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring player engagement or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Necromancer's domain and witch's castle environments
danger_signals.meaning: Major quest obstacles and boss territories marked by dark/corrupted visuals
Loss of active brother; Luck stat consumed to revive, forcing player to switch characters

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone and quest origin point; talisman recovery destination

safety_signals.visual_form: Gathered artifacts and NPC aid (sea turtle, rescued king's daughter)
safety_signals.meaning: Progression toward necromancer's dimension access; world state improvement

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible stat increases (Bravery → Vitality gains, Kindness accumulation)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Necromancer entity, hostile creatures, witch in castle form
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Necromancer's dimension (strange/otherworldly space), witch's castle (corrupted stronghold)
Tambry village (friendly territory), king's court (allied nobility), sea locations (sea turtle ally)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Evil presence manifest in game world; quest objectives marked by scattered locations (castle, dimension access)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must traverse 17,000+ screens seeking specific objectives; enemies active in dangerous zones

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Objective completion (save king's daughter, gain sea turtle aid, kill witch)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression markers visible through artifact collection; dimension entrance becomes accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlocked access to necromancer's strange dimension; world becomes navigable toward final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Active brother incapacitated; control shifts to next sibling (Julian → Phillip → Kevin)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Luck stat consumed per revival; limited resurrection attempts force character rotation strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return to Tambry village with talisman object
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game objective completed; world threat neutralized

environmental_storytelling.example: Overworld divided into zones with narrative purpose: Tambry (safe home), king's court (allied authority), witch's castle (corrupted stronghold), necromancer's dimension (final evil stronghold). Each location visually signals its role in the larger quest narrative.
Collectible artifacts scattered across world serve dual function: mechanical (dimension access key) and narrative (evidence of quest progression; visual markers of completed objectives)
King's daughter, sea turtle, and beggars positioned at narrative waypoints; interaction with these entities unlocks world state changes and signals quest advancement

Three brothers' distinct personalities (Julian=Brave/Vitality, Phillip=Lucky/Resurrection, Kevin=Kind/Social) represented through stat mechanics; kindness acts toward beggars directly influence character capability, linking moral choice to mechanical progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: High Bravery contributes to Vitality; suited for enemy slaying; primary fighter role
High Luck enables multiple resurrections; best survival rate across death/revival cycles
High Kindness unlocks story chapters and NPC interactions; essential for non-combat objectives (beggars, moral choices)
Real-time combat engagement; killable for Bravery stat gains; presence indicates danger zones
Confined to strange dimension; requires artifact collection and objective completion before confrontation; ultimate goal manifestation
Located in castle stronghold; must be slain as mandatory objective; unlock condition for dimensional access

notes: The Faery Tale Adventure uses character attribute mechanics (Bravery/Vitality, Luck/Resurrection, Kindness/Story Access) to visually and behaviorally communicate world state through character stat manifestation. The massive world (17,000+ screens) serves as environmental storytelling mechanism—quest objectives scattered across geography force player navigation and discovery. Death/resurrection mechanics tied to Luck stat create visual progression of character depletion (Julian→Phillip→Kevin rotation). Evil faction presence is localized to specific strongholds (witch's castle, necromancer's dimension) rather than pervasive, creating safe/danger zone binary. The talisman serves as central visual/narrative anchor across all world states—its theft initiates quest; its recovery ends narrative.
